input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest SDK version
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install (adjust version as needed)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls Android SDK, build tools, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Java JDK (required for Android)

```powershell
# MAUI installs Microsoft OpenJDK automatically via the android workload.
# Confirm location:
dotnet android --info

# If missing, install manually:
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --silent

# Set JAVA_HOME if needed
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17.*", "User")
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The `maui` workload auto-installs the Android SDK. To verify or customize:

```powershell
# Default location
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# Discover installed platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific API level dynamically (e.g., latest stable)
$available = & "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1
$available | Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install what you need
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" "platform-tools"

# Accept licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Android Emulator (optional but recommended)

```powershell
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "emulator" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"
```

## 6. Enable Windows App SDK target (for WinUI3)

```powershell
# Usually included with MAUI workload. Confirm:
dotnet workload list | Select-String "maui-windows"

# Install Windows App SDK if missing
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK --silent

# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 7. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Discover available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create project
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet run -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 8. Discover Target Frameworks Dynamically

```powershell
# See what TFMs your SDK supports
dotnet --list-runtimes
dotnet new maui --dry-run | Select-String "TargetFrameworks"

# Check the generated .csproj for exact TFMs
Select-Xml -Path *.csproj -XPath "//TargetFrameworks" |
  ForEach-Object { $_.Node.InnerText }
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if using .NET 9 SDK — use `dotnet --version` to check.
- The `dotnet workload install maui` command handles most dependencies (Android SDK, build tools, OpenJDK).
- Run PowerShell as **Administrator** for the registry change and workload installs.
